text: 5. A particle moves on the cartesian plane in such a way that from point (a, b) it can go to either (a + 1, b) or (a, b + 1). Given n ∈ N, let an be the number of distinct ways the particle has to go from Ao (O, O) to An (n, n), without ever touching a point (x, y) situated above the bisector of odd quadrants (i.e., one such point for which y > x). In this respect, do the following items:
